title: Cyclosorus inclusus
taxonomy: Cyclosorus inclusus is classified within the plant family Thelypteridaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh fern family. This family is part of the larger group known as the Polypodiales, an order of flowering plants that are actually ferns. The order Polypodiales is a part of the division Pteridophyta, which includes all types of ferns. The Pteridophyta division is further divided into several classes, with Cyclosorus inclusus belonging to the class Polypodiopsida. This classification indicates the plant's evolutionary relationships and its position within the plant kingdom.
uses: Cyclosorus inclusus, also known as the included cyclosorus fern, is used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. In some cultures, it is used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves are also used in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Cyclosorus inclusus for medicinal purposes.

intro: Cyclosorus inclusus, also known as the Inclusive Lady fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. The health benefits of Cyclosorus inclusus include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate various health conditions such as arthritis and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Cyclosorus inclusus is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as tea, capsules, and extracts, which are often used to treat digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems.
